title: Comptime — The Code That Writes Code
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Zig's comptime executes arbitrary code at compile time, eliminating runtime branches and generating specialized code for each call site
content.proof: comptime parameters are evaluated by the compiler. fn max(comptime T: type, a: T, b: T) T — the compiler generates a separate function for each T used. No monomorphization overhead, no vtable, no generics complexity. It's just code that runs during compilation.
content.benchmark: Comptime-specialized snap: 2.05B ops/sec. Equivalent runtime-dispatched: 1.1B ops/sec. The branch elimination is worth nearly 2×.
content.caveat: Comptime execution is single-threaded. Heavy comptime usage slows compilation. Balance: use comptime for type specialization, not heavy computation.
